evidence: package.json declares private application 'maas-portal-vue' with dev/build/preview scripts — confirming standalone deployable SPA
11 defined routes in src/router/index.ts with auth guards indicate complete multi-page application with protected workflows
13 portal business components under src/components/portal/ implement specific MaaS domain logic (dashboard, packages, orders, services, models, billing, audit, enterprise, accounts, wallet)
40 Radix Vue wrapper components under src/components/ui/ form a reusable UI component library within the application
src/stores/auth.ts implements complete auth flow with demo user login, registration, session persistence via sessionStorage, and logout
src/main.ts serves as the application bootstrap — initializes Pinia store, restores auth session, registers router, and mounts the Vue app
No library/framework export patterns found (no index.ts barrel exports, no plugin registration API, no build-to-library config)
Tailwind CSS v4 integrated via @tailwindcss/vite plugin with class-variance-authority for component variant management
Vite configured with Vue plugin, Tailwind plugin, and @/ path alias mapping to ./src — standard SPA build configuration
README.md documents core business modules with Chinese localization, confirming enterprise MaaS portal positioning
summary: MaaS Portal Vue is an enterprise-grade single-page application (SPA) built with Vue 3 + TypeScript, serving as a Model-as-a-Service management portal. The application provides a complete front-end interface for enterprise users to manage MaaS service packages, orders, service instances, models, billing, wallets, audit logs, enterprise information, and sub-account permissions. It features 11 business routes with authentication guards, a Pinia-based auth store with session persistence, a comprehensive UI component library (40 components) built on Radix Vue, and a collapsible sidebar layout. The project uses Vite as the build tool with Tailwind CSS v4 for styling. Currently at v0.1.0 with mock/demo authentication and no backend API integration, representing a front-end prototype or early-stage application.
